# Feature: Run Reporting — Test Suite Run HTML Report

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-005
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_run_reporting.md`

Add an "Export Report" button to [TestResultsPanel.tsx](packages/core/src/components/testlab/TestResultsPanel.tsx) that, on click, builds a single-file HTML report of the latest test-suite run and downloads it via the `exportFile` adapter (FEAT-001). The report mirrors the panel's full nesting:

- A **Request** test item expands to show test cases (data-driven), each test case rendered through the shared `renderRequestDetail` block.
- A **Flow** test item expands to either (a) test cases (each test case shows the flow's node list) or (b) the flow's node list directly when there are no test cases — same branching as the panel.

This is the final feature of the run reporting plan and the most structurally complex builder. It reuses everything from FEAT-002 / FEAT-003 / FEAT-004 and adds only the test-suite-specific tree builder + UI wiring.

---

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

`.spec-lite/data_model.md` does not exist. This feature adds one new builder input type and one new method on the existing `useReportExport` hook.

### Entities & Attributes

- **`TestSuiteReportInput`** (new type in `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/builders/testSuiteRun.ts`)
  - `metadata` (`ReportMetadata`) — `runType: 'testsuite'`, `subjectName: suite.name`, `concurrentCalls` / `delayBetweenCalls` are `undefined`, `startedAt` / `completedAt` from `TestSuiteRunResult`, `totalElapsedMs` derived, `environmentName` / `defaultAuthName` `undefined` (same TODO.md item as the flow report).
  - `summary` (`ReportSummary`) — derived from `TestSuiteRunResult.progress` (`passed`, `failed`, `skipped`, `total`) and `averageTime`.
  - `items` (`TestSuiteReportItem[]`) — discriminated union, one entry per enabled `TestItem` ordered by `item.order`:
    - `{ kind: 'request'; name: string; status: RunStatus; testCases?: ReportRequestNode[]; single?: ReportRequestNode }` — when the item has test cases (`testCaseResults.size > 0`), `testCases` is populated; otherwise `single` is the single request execution.
    - `{ kind: 'flow'; name: string; status: RunStatus; testCases?: { id: string; name: string; status: RunStatus; nodes: ReportRequestNode[] }[]; nodes?: ReportRequestNode[] }` — when the flow has test cases, `testCases` is populated (each carrying its own resolved node list); otherwise `nodes` is the single-run node list.

- **`useReportExport` extension** (final extension)
  - New method `exportTestSuiteRun(input: TestSuiteReportInput): Promise<void>` with the same status-machine semantics as the previous two methods.

### Relationships

- N/A.

### Indexes & Constraints

- The Export button is disabled when (a) `result === null`, (b) `result.status === 'running'`, or (c) `reportStatus.state === 'generating'`.
- All node / request resolution from `collections` and `flows` arrays happens in the *panel* (TASK-003) before invoking the builder, so the builder receives only resolved `ReportRequestNode` data and stays platform-agnostic.

---

- `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/builders/testSuiteRun.ts` — `buildTestSuiteRunReport(input: TestSuiteReportInput): string` plus internal renderers for the test-item / test-case / flow-node nesting.

## 4. Dependencies

- **FEAT-001** — `IStorageAdapter.exportFile`.
- **FEAT-002** — Foundation module.
- **FEAT-003** — `useReportExport` hook (extended in this feature).
- **FEAT-004** — Optional but recommended; sharing the `buildReportFilename` + status-machine pattern is established by then. No code dependency on FEAT-004.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Implement `buildTestSuiteRunReport` with nested rendering

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/builders/testSuiteRun.ts`, implement `buildTestSuiteRunReport(input)` composing `renderShell({ title: \`Wave Test Suite Run — ${metadata.subjectName}\`, body })`. `body = renderHeader(metadata) + renderSummary(summary) + renderItems(items)`. `renderItems` produces one collapsible block per `TestSuiteReportItem`: an outer card (with method/flow icon — use a non-method visual badge for flow items, since flows do not have an HTTP method) showing item name + aggregate status; expanding the card reveals one of four nested layouts based on the discriminant: (a) `request` + `single` → render via `renderRequestDetail`. (b) `request` + `testCases` → render a "Test Cases" header + summary tally + each test case via `renderRequestDetail`. (c) `flow` + `nodes` → render a node list via `renderRequestDetail` for each node. (d) `flow` + `testCases` → render a "Test Cases" header + each test case as a *nested* collapsible block whose expanded body lists the test case's `nodes` via `renderRequestDetail`. All collapsible behavior reuses the same `data-toggle="card"` / `data-card-body` convention from `INTERACTIVITY_JS` — no new JS is added. All dynamic strings go through `escapeHtml` / `escapeAttr`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `testSuiteRun.test.ts` covers each of the four item shapes plus an "all enabled items" suite: (1) request item with single result. (2) request item with 3 data-driven test cases (mix of pass / fail) — assert all three sub-cards are present with correct status badges. (3) flow item with node list (no test cases). (4) flow item with 2 test cases, each with its own resolved node list — assert nesting depth. (5) Items are ordered by their `order` field and only `enabled` items appear (the panel filters before calling, but the builder must not re-order or filter — assert the input order is preserved). (6) Empty `items` → valid shell + zero-summary tiles. (7) XSS regression: inject `<script>` into suite name, item name, test case name, and a node name — assert literal escaped form, no executable `<script>`.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the builder + input types, including the discriminated-union shape diagram for items. Update `packages/core/src/utils/reporting/index.ts`.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- reporting/builders/testSuiteRun` passes. Manual smoke: open a produced report from a real test-suite run and confirm clicks expand all nesting levels correctly.

### TASK-002: Extend `useReportExport` with `exportTestSuiteRun`

- [ ] **Implementation**: Add `exportTestSuiteRun(input: TestSuiteReportInput): Promise<void>` mirroring the previous two methods: `idle → generating → success | error`, calls `buildTestSuiteRunReport(input)` and `adapter.storage.exportFile(buildReportFilename('testsuite', metadata.subjectName, new Date()), html, 'text/html')`. Same error/notification contract.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Extend `useReportExport.test.tsx` to cover the test-suite path: (1) successful export with the right filename prefix (`wave-testsuite-...`). (2) Adapter error surfaces via notification. (3) Multiple sequential exports of different types (collection → flow → testsuite) all transition cleanly through the state machine.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the new method. Update the hook-level JSDoc to enumerate all three supported export entry points.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- useReportExport` passes.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001.

### TASK-003: Wire Export button into `TestResultsPanel`

- [ ] **Implementation**: In [TestResultsPanel.tsx](packages/core/src/components/testlab/TestResultsPanel.tsx), add an Export button to the same row as the existing Clear Results control (top of the header section). Lucide `DownloadIcon`, `Button variant="ghost" size="sm"`, tooltip "Export HTML report". Disabled when `result === null || result.status === 'running' || reportStatus.state === 'generating'`. While generating, swap the icon for a spinning `Loader2`. On click, build a `TestSuiteReportInput` by walking `suite.items` (filtered to `enabled`, sorted by `order`) and resolving each item's `ReportRequestNode`s using the same `findRequestMeta` + buildRunRequestData-style helpers already present in the panel: (a) For each request item, if `testCaseResults` exists and is non-empty, build one `ReportRequestNode` per test case via the existing `convertTestCaseToRunRequestData` helper (already exported / inlined in the panel); else build a single `ReportRequestNode`. (b) For each flow item, look up the matching `Flow` from the `flows` prop, then for each test case (if any) walk `flow.nodes` and resolve to `ReportRequestNode[]` (reuse the same `findRequestMeta` + node-result merge from the existing `FlowResultSubPanel` helper); else resolve a single node list. Pass the assembled input to `useReportExport().exportTestSuiteRun(input)`. After success, show a transient check icon for ~3 s.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `TestResultsPanel.test.tsx` extensions: (1) Export button renders. (2) Disabled when `result === null`. (3) Disabled when `result.status === 'running'`. (4) Click invokes `exportTestSuiteRun` once with input whose `items` length matches the count of enabled items. (5) Mixed-shape suite (request item with test cases + request item without + flow item with test cases + flow item without) produces an input with the correct discriminants per item. (6) On error, the error notification surface is called.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the panel's new export-related local state. Inline comment near the input-building block noting which existing helpers are reused (`findRequestMeta`, `convertTestCaseToRunRequestData`, the flow node walker from `FlowResultSubPanel`).
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- TestResultsPanel` passes. Manual smoke: run a test suite that mixes request items + flow items + data-driven test cases, click Export, open the report and click through every level of nesting to confirm interactivity.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002.

---

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: Same as the flow report — `defaultAuthName` is `undefined` until runners surface the resolved auth name (tracked in `TODO.md`). The header block omits the row gracefully when undefined.
- **Error Handling**: Reuses the hook's `Result<T, E>` + notification adapter contract from FEAT-003. The builder is pure and never throws.
- **Logging**: N/A.
